title: Rose Byrne Wins Golden Globe for Best Actress in Musical or Comedy
text: Rose Byrne claimed her first Golden Globe award at Sunday's ceremony for her role in the psychological thriller "If I Had Legs I'd Kick You," where she portrays a mother experiencing mental distress. Byrne defeated fellow nominees Cynthia Erivo, Kate Hudson, Chase Infiniti, Amanda Seyfried, and Emma Stone in the Best Actress in a Musical or Comedy category. Byrne expressed genuine surprise at winning. She joked about the dark nature of her film's categorization, saying: "I didn't sing in this movie. This is such a shock!" She praised filmmaker Mary Bronstein and noted the production's impressive efficiency: "We shot this movie in 25 days for, like, $8.50. This is a tiny film, so this is a huge thing to be up here." In a humorous moment, Byrne revealed why her brother accompanied her instead of her longtime partner Bobby Cannavale: "he couldn't be here because we are getting a bearded dragon, and he went to a reptile expo in New Jersey." "If I Had Legs I'd Kick You" follows Linda as she deteriorates mentally while her husband is away and her mysteriously ill daughter remains attached to medical equipment. The ensemble cast includes Conan O'Brien, Christian Slater, and A$AP Rocky. The film premiered at Sundance and earned Byrne a Silver Bear at the Berlin Film Festival.
